mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term equity momentum and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0 percent over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and HOUST rising simultaneously fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise borrowing costs and suppress corporate profit margins, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0 percent and rises consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings base for public corporations, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as analysts revise forecasts upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 acceleration and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105.0, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, signaling strong corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of calendar months where INDPRO trades above 105.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls paired with declining unemployment rates validate a self-reinforcing labor demand shock, which sustains consumer credit expansion and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS increasing and UNRATE decreasing fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion environment, housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units signal overheating construction activity that triggers anticipatory Federal Reserve rate hikes, depressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of weeks where HOUST surpasses 1200.0 during a late-cycle expansion phase.
